notes_md:
**thu** usually means **autumn / fall**, the season between summer and winter. In daily speech, you almost always say **mùa thu** (mùa = season), not just *thu* by itself.

It’s an uncountable season noun, like other seasons: **xuân – hạ – thu – đông**. No classifier needed.

Very common second meaning: **thu** = **to collect / receive / gather** (money, tax, garbage, tickets, etc.).  

Easy memory trick: *mùa thu* = fall (the season), but *thu tiền* = collect money — same spelling, different job.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Every year, I really like to walk in the park in autumn.
She usually drinks hot tea in the morning when autumn comes.
I love the cool feeling and the falling yellow leaves in autumn.

definitions.en: To gather or collect, especially crops or fruits.
definitions.es: Reunir o recoger, especialmente cultivos o frutas.
definitions.vi: Thu thập hoặc thu hoạch, đặc biệt là cây trồng hoặc trái cây.

example.vi: Tôi sẽ thu hoạch rau vào cuối tuần.
example.en: I will harvest the vegetables this weekend.
example.es: Cosecharé las verduras este fin de semana.

exampleSentences: Mỗi năm, anh rất thích đi dạo trong công viên vào mùa thu.
Chị thường uống trà nóng vào buổi sáng khi trời sang thu.
Em yêu cảm giác mát mẻ và lá vàng rơi vào mùa thu.
notes:

It’s an uncountable season noun, like other seasons: xuân – hạ – thu – đông. No classifier needed.

Very common second meaning: thu = to collect / receive / gather (money, tax, garbage, tickets, etc.).  

Easy memory trick: mùa thu = fall (the season), but thu tiền = collect money — same spelling, different job.
